behaviors.description: Summary-generation input includes data from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff (three collectors -> aggregator -> summary input), not a single pure function (`MOCK-MISS`). Real OS/network behavior is not required to prove this; stubbed source clients can still expose missing/overwritten section wiring (`REAL-INFRA`, `MOCK-HIDE`). No dependence on model output quality, only on assembled input payload (`LLM-DEP`). Considered `System` due API sources, but rejected because behavior text is about inclusion in passed content, not real HTTP semantics/timeouts.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/orchestration modules with three deterministic in-process source fakes returning distinct markers. Execute one report run and assert the exact payload passed to the summary-generation boundary contains all three source sections/keys.

behaviors.description: If one source is unavailable, report is still produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience across module boundaries: source client error propagation -> report composer fallback (`MOCK-MISS`). In-process failure injection (exception/timeout object from one client fake) can reproduce the real branch; no mandatory OS/network primitive is needed (`REAL-INFRA`). LLM quality is not the correctness target; fallback note presence is (`LLM-DEP`). Considered `System` for real outage behavior, rejected because behavior text requires graceful degradation logic, which is testable via deterministic module interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Run report pipeline with two source fakes returning data and one fake raising an unavailability error. Assert run completes, output report exists, and unavailable section contains `data unavailable` while other sections are intact.

behaviors.description: Narrative summary accurately represents key values from input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the summarizer actually generates, not just routing/payload format (`LLM-DEP`). A stubbed summarizer would always pass and hide factuality/omission errors (`MOCK-HIDE`). No multi-agent sequence is stated; behavior references one summary-generation step, so `Workflow` is not required (`MULTI-STEP` rejection evidence: singular summary behavior).
behaviors.test_description: Call the real summary-generation model with representative report inputs containing known key metrics/tickets/deploy counts. Evaluate output for factual alignment to those key values (no contradictions, required values present) across repeated runs/tolerance thresholds.

behaviors.description: End-to-end report flow (collect -> summarize -> email) reaches configured recipient inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external delivery path and inbox receipt, which requires real HTTP/SMTP/service behavior (`REAL-INFRA`). In-process mocks can hide auth, transport, provider acceptance, and mailbox routing failures (`MOCK-HIDE`). Considered `Agentic` because summary step may be model-backed; rejected as minimum level since behavior text validates delivery to inbox, not model decision quality. `Workflow` rejected: no requirement for multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full job in an environment using real source endpoints (or production-like reachable services) and real email delivery provider toward a test mailbox. Assert one complete report email is received by configured recipient with expected structural sections.

behaviors.description: If email service is unreachable, save report locally and retry delivery on next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state handoff across runs: delivery failure handling -> file persistence -> retry loader/sender (`MOCK-MISS`). This can be reproduced with real local filesystem and deterministic failing email adapter, then a second invocation to represent next run; no mandatory real scheduler daemon or real network outage is required (`REAL-INFRA`). Considered `System` due unreachable service/scheduling wording, rejected because behavior text does not require validating cron/SMTP stack itself, only fallback persistence and retry semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run job twice against a temp local storage path: first run uses email adapter that fails unreachable and asserts report file persisted; second run uses successful adapter and asserts persisted report is retried/sent and retry artifact is cleared/marked delivered.
